HomePage.featureModulesBody: From foundations to advanced agentic workflows.

HomePage.featureProjectsBody: Track practice outputs, self-review checklists, and review states.
HomePage.featureAdminBody: Manage learners, content, and progress from one control plane.

ToolsPage.title: Choose the right AI tool for the task instead of chasing the “strongest” one
ToolsPage.description: The most effective approach is to match the tool to the task shape, context depth, and verification needs. Use this page as a quick decision aid.
ToolsPage.boltFit: Fast visual prototypes from rough ideas
ToolsPage.boltStrengths: Quick to start and great for turning text into demoable UI.
ToolsPage.boltWatchouts: Best for early exploration, not long-term complex code maintenance.
ToolsPage.cursorFit: High-frequency AI collaboration inside the editor
ToolsPage.cursorStrengths: Smooth for local edits, completions, diffs, and rapid iteration.
ToolsPage.cursorWatchouts: Multi-step work still needs you to manage the rhythm and boundaries.
ToolsPage.claudeFit: Continuous implementation and verification in the terminal
ToolsPage.claudeStrengths: Strong at reading repos, making changes, and running verification while keeping context aligned.
ToolsPage.claudeWatchouts: Needs clearer goals, constraints, and verification criteria.
ToolsPage.codexFit: Strong repo understanding and task execution
ToolsPage.codexStrengths: Works well when moving through specs, task lists, and verification outcomes.
ToolsPage.codexWatchouts: If the context boundary is vague, scope can drift too wide.
ToolsPage.windsurfStrengths: Useful for fast page assembly and experience-oriented frontend exploration.
ToolsPage.windsurfWatchouts: Before serious engineering work, you still need stronger specs and validation.
ToolsPage.commandTypecheck: `npm run typecheck` check type safety first
ToolsPage.commandBuild: `npm run build` run a full production build check
ToolsPage.commandGenerate: `npx prisma generate` refresh the client after schema changes
